title: Node-fetch requires ESM instead of CommonJS
symptom: The workflow fails with an error indicating that 'require() of ES Module' is not supported. This occurs after updating n8n and using node-fetch version 3.x.
root_cause: Node-fetch version 3.x is an ES Module and cannot be imported using CommonJS's require() syntax. This change in module format can lead to compatibility issues when used in environments expecting CommonJS modules.
fix_steps: Update your package.json or EXTRA_NODE_MODULES environment variable accordingly
agent_instruction: Downgrade node-fetch to version 2.7.0 to resolve the ESM compatibility issue.
